{"decision":"new_watchpoint","confidence":0.62,"abstention_reason":null,"summary":"Between the 2025-11-05 and 2026-02-04 AMD filings, the demand/supply-chain/inventory cyclicality thesis appears largely reiterated, but a new, more concrete geopolitical/export-controls watchpoint emerges: a disclosed ~$800M inventory-related charge tied to U.S. export restrictions on MI308 data center GPUs to China, partially mitigated by licenses to ship to certain customers.","rationale":"","evidence_refs":["sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-25-000166#part-ii-item-1a:theme:demand:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-25-000166#part-ii-item-1a:theme:supply-chain:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-25-000166#part-ii-item-1a:theme:inventory-channel:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-26-000018#item-1a:theme:demand:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-26-000018#item-1a:theme:supply-chain:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-26-000018#item-1a:theme:inventory-channel:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-26-000018#item-7:theme:geopolitics-tariffs:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-26-000018#item-7:theme:inventory-channel:outcome","sec-company://0000002488/0000002488-26-000018#item-7:theme:cloud-data-center-capacity:outcome"],"uncertainty":null,"recommended_next_step":"Create a dedicated watchpoint for export-controls exposure in data center GPUs (MI308/China): in the next 10-Q/10-K, check for (1) additional charges or reversals, (2) updated license coverage/denials, (3) segment-level revenue/margin commentary tied to restricted SKUs, and (4) any explicit changes to China/data-center demand outlook or inventory policy tied to export compliance."}

{"decision":"new_watchpoint","confidence":0.62,"abstention_reason":null,"summary":"Across the two 2026 10-Qs, Microsoft reiterates that operating expense growth is being driven by (1) Gaming impairment/related expenses and (2) ongoing investments in AI-related R&D inputs (compute capacity, AI talent, and data). The Apr 29, 2026 filing again attributes a 6% opex increase, but with a larger cited absolute increase ($628M), to Gaming impairment/related expenses and continued investments in R&D compute capacity, AI talent, and data. This indicates persistence of the same drivers and suggests the scale of expense growth associated with these drivers may be rising, warranting explicit monitoring rather than concluding the underlying thesis strengthened or weakened.","evidence_refs":["sec-company://0000789019/0001193125-26-027207#part-i-item-2:theme:cloud-data-center-capacity:outcome","sec-company://0000789019/0001193125-26-027207#part-i-item-2:theme:ai-rd-technology:outcome","sec-company://0000789019/0001193125-26-191507#part-i-item-2:theme:cloud-data-center-capacity:outcome","sec-company://0000789019/0001193125-26-191507#part-i-item-2:theme:ai-rd-technology:outcome"],"uncertainty":"Only excerpt-level outcome statements are provided, not the full Item 2 context or segmented expense detail. The $236M vs $628M figures may refer to different comparable periods, expense line items, or consolidation scopes, and the excerpts do not show offsetting factors (revenue/margin, capitalized costs, or productivity gains). Without the thesis’ specific claims (e.g., margin expansion vs investment phase), the directional impact on the thesis cannot be confidently scored as strengthened or weakened.","recommended_next_step":"Extract the full Part I, Item 2 MD&A expense discussion and relevant financial statement notes from both filings (2026-01-28 and 2026-04-29) to (1) reconcile the $236M and $628M opex deltas to specific lines (R&D vs S&M vs G&A), (2) isolate Gaming impairment amounts and drivers, and (3) quantify AI-related spend indicators (compute/data center costs, headcount/talent, capitalized software/PP&E where discussed) to set explicit monitoring thresholds for opex and impairment recurrence."}

{"decision":"thesis_unchanged","confidence":0.66,"abstention_reason":null,"summary":"Across the two filings, the core thesis evidence is consistent: U.S. export licensing requirements for H20 to China led to diminished demand and a $4.5B charge for excess inventory/purchase obligations, with licenses later granted (Aug 2025) to ship certain H20 products to certain China-based customers. The only observed change is omission of an incremental metric (\"~$50M revenue to date under licenses\") in the later excerpt; omission alone does not falsify improvement or deterioration and could reflect materiality, reporting period context, or relocation of detail elsewhere in the filing.","evidence_refs":["sec-company://0001045810/0001045810-25-000230#part-i-item-2:theme:demand:outcome","sec-company://0001045810/0001045810-25-000230#part-i-item-2:theme:inventory-channel:outcome","sec-company://0001045810/0001045810-26-000021#item-7:theme:demand:outcome","sec-company://0001045810/0001045810-26-000021#item-7:theme:inventory-channel:outcome"],"uncertainty":"It is unclear whether revenue under the granted licenses increased, decreased, or became immaterial, because the later excerpt does not restate the earlier \"~$50M to date\" figure and no alternative updated metric is provided in the supplied evidence. The delta may be purely disclosure placement/wording rather than a business change.","recommended_next_step":"Create a watchpoint to track (a) any updated quantified revenue/shipments under China licenses for H20 (or successor products), (b) any additional inventory/purchase-obligation charges or reversals tied to H20, and (c) any new/expanded USG licensing restrictions or allowances in subsequent 10-Q/10-K risk factors and MD&A."}

{"decision":"new_watchpoint","confidence":0.62,"abstention_reason":null,"summary":"Latest filing evidence indicates improved consumer net loan charge-offs year-over-year, attributed to lower credit card portfolio losses; this creates a watchpoint on whether consumer/credit-card credit normalization is durable and how it interacts with liquidity and demand conditions.","rationale":"The cited outcome shows consumer net loan charge-offs decreasing in 1Q 2026 versus the prior-year period, explicitly driven by lower credit card losses. The improvement could be transient (mix shift, seasonality, accounting/charge-off timing, or macro inflection), and the magnitude relative to overall portfolio risk is not provided here.","recommended_next_step":"Add a falsifiable watchpoint for the next filing(s): track consumer (especially credit card) net charge-offs and leading indicators (delinquencies, nonperforming assets, and provision trends) and assess whether the year-over-year improvement persists or reverses, with explicit linkage to any stated demand and liquidity conditions in Management’s Discussion and Risk Factors."}
